name: Design System Heretics
description: Designers who rage against the machine of bloated UI, unmaintainable state logic, and the tyranny of 10-step forms. These are the ones who see spatial dissonance and cognitive load as personal insults.

name: Accessibility & FOSS Advocates
description: Engineers and designers who champion open-source tools and accessibility standards in digital product design, ensuring systems are inclusive and maintainable.

name: Behavioral Psychology in Frontend Engineering
description: Engineers and designers who translate cognitive load, Fitts's Law, and Hick's Law into component architecture, state management, and interaction design. They rage against the machine of bloated UI but do it with spatial mathematics and DOM-level precision.

name: Systems Thinkers in Blockchain UX
description: Engineers and designers who dissect the invisible UI of on-chain interactions, translating cryptographic complexity into frictionless frontend experiences. name: Behavioral Psychology in Frontend Engineering
description: Engineers and designers who translate cognitive load, Fitts's Law, Hick's Law, and Miller's Law into frontend systems that don't make users' brains crash like a tab with 500 open WebSockets.

name: Frontend Engineering Heretics
description: Designers and engineers who rage against the machine of bloated UI, unmaintainable state logic, and DOM spaghetti. They dissect the structural rot in modern frontend architectures and prescribe systemic solutions rooted in behavioral psychology and spatial mathematics.

name: Pragmatic Design Engineers in Web3
description: Engineers and designers who dissect the invisible UI of on-chain interactions, translating behavioral psychology into scalable component architecture for decentralized systems.
